clinical_history: 60 year-old female with complaints of chest pain and shortness of breath. The ECG shows which of the following?
diagnosis:
This ECG shows sinus rhythm at 72 bpm with left bundle branch block (LBBB) with QRS duration 0.16 second, normal AV conduction (PR.0.17 second) and a QT interval at the upper limits of normal (0.42 second.) 

This patient had a history of hypertension and idiopathic cardiomyopathy with a left ventricular ejection fraction by echocardiography of 35%. She ruled out for a myocardial infarction. Serum potassium was normal. While acute MI cannot be ruled out by ECG alone in this context, the findings of tall right precordial T waves and J point elevations of this magnitude are consistent entirely with left bundle branch block.
